---
title: "SDPD-2"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-20"
---

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

# Leer datos
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/Javi/Downloads/reviews.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Quitar duplicados por id
df <- df %>% distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Eliminar filas con NA reales
df <- df %>% filter(complete.cases(.))

# Eliminar filas completamente duplicadas
df <- df %>% distinct()

# Limpiar espacios en texto
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    reviewer_name = trimws(reviewer_name),
    comments = trimws(comments),
    comments = gsub("[\r\n]+", " ", comments),
    comments = gsub("\\s+", " ", comments)
  )

# Convertir IDs a character para evitar overflow
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    listing_id = as.character(listing_id),
    id = as.character(id),
    reviewer_id = as.character(reviewer_id),
    reviewer_name = as.character(reviewer_name),
    comments = as.character(comments),
    date = as.Date(date)
  )

# Comprobaciones finales
sum(is.na(df))
sum(duplicated(df$id))
sum(duplicated(df))
str(df)
dim(df)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Crear variables auxiliares para visualizar
df_plot <- df %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    comment_length = nchar(comments)
  )

# 1. Reviews por fecha
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribución temporal de las reviews",
    x = "Fecha",
    y = "Número de reviews"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 2. Longitud de los comentarios
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = comment_length)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribución de la longitud de los comentarios",
    x = "Número de caracteres",
    y = "Frecuencia"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3. Top 10 listings con más reviews
top_listings <- df_plot %>%
  count(listing_id, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

ggplot(top_listings, aes(x = reorder(listing_id, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 listings con más reviews",
    x = "Listing ID",
    y = "Número de reviews"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 4. Top 10 reviewers más activos
top_reviewers <- df_plot %>%
  count(reviewer_id, reviewer_name, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

ggplot(top_reviewers, aes(x = reorder(reviewer_name, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 reviewers más activos",
    x = "Reviewer",
    y = "Número de reviews"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 5. Boxplot de longitud de comentarios
ggplot(df_plot, aes(y = comment_length)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Boxplot de la longitud de los comentarios",
    y = "Número de caracteres"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
